metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the leaf's dual-schema strategy (`fail_v1` + `pass_v1`) with the parent epic's explicit mandate of a `completion_*_v2` schema bump: either adopt the parent's `_v2` versioning approach (one schema covering PASS records, readers accepting both `v1` and `v2`), or update the parent epic's Compatibility section first to authorize the dual-schema approach before re-reviewing the leaf.
findings.finding: The leaf's schema-versioning strategy contradicts the parent epic's stated approach. The parent (ba33 Compatibility section) explicitly says completion sidecar schemas are 'bumped' to a '_v2' with readers accepting both old v1 and new v2; the leaf instead keeps SCHEMA='completion_verifier_fail_v1' unchanged for FAILs and introduces a separate SCHEMA_PASS='completion_verifier_pass_v1', explicitly prohibiting the _v2 bump the parent mandates.
findings.location: Leaf: Schema-version strategy section; Parent epic ba33: Compatibility & rollback section
findings.suggested_fix: Align the leaf to the parent: introduce `SCHEMA_PASS = 'completion_verifier_pass_v1'` renamed to `SCHEMA_V2 = 'completion_verifier_pass_v2'` (or `completion_verifier_v2`) and add a v1-compatible reader that accepts both schemas, matching the parent's 'readers accept BOTH old v1 and new v2 records' contract. Alternatively, update ba33's Compatibility section to explicitly authorize the dual-schema approach (fail_v1 preserved, new pass_v1 additive) and re-review the leaf.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `@field_validator("kind")` to the `Criterion` sub-model that normalizes to the closed set `{"codebase-verifiable", "operator-attested"}` (coerce case/whitespace; map unknown values to `"codebase-verifiable"` as the safe default), mirroring the `_coerce_kind` and `_norm_relation` patterns already in `contracts.py`.
findings.finding: `Criterion.kind` is declared as bare `kind: str` with no normalizing validator, despite the plan defining it as a CLOSED two-value enum. Every other closed-enum field in the codebase (CompletionVerdict.verdict via `_norm_verdict`, Citation.kind via `_coerce_kind`, OverlapVerdict.relation via `_norm_relation`) has a normalizing validator that coerces out-of-vocabulary values. Without one, an LLM emitting `"codebase_verifiable"`, `"CODEBASE-VERIFIABLE"`, or any near-miss will store an invalid `kind` in the sidecar undetected.
findings.location: Scope section — `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py` edit, `Criterion` sub-model definition: `{criterion: str, met: bool, citation: Citation | None, kind: str}`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in the `build_payload` edit that the PASS-branch payload includes `"criteria": v.get("criteria") or []` (reading from the post-reconcile shallow copy `v`, which carries the field through since `reconcile_verdict` does not touch it), making it parallel to how `"findings"` is already read from `v`.
findings.finding: `build_payload` in `completion_sidecar.py` calls `reconcile_verdict(v)` on a shallow copy of the verdict dict before constructing the payload. The plan says the PASS branch should produce `SCHEMA_PASS + criteria[]`. However `reconcile_verdict` does not currently carry `criteria` (it only touches `verdict`, `findings`, and `remediation`). The plan does not specify whether `build_payload` reads `criteria` from the original `verdict` argument or from the post-reconcile copy `v`, nor whether `reconcile_verdict` should be updated to preserve `criteria` pass-through. If `criteria` is only in `verdict` (original) and `v` is a shallow copy, `criteria` IS present on `v` since a shallow copy includes all keys — but the plan's `build_payload` branch description only says 'PASS → `SCHEMA_PASS` + `criteria[]`' without specifying the source (`v.get("criteria")` or `verdict.get("criteria")`).
findings.suggested_fix: In the `build_payload` PASS branch: `"criteria": v.get("criteria") or [],` — mirroring the existing `"findings": v.get("findings", []) or []` pattern.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `material` value for the PASS-path `completion_sidecar.emit` call — either explicitly `material=None` (matching the FAIL path, with a note that the fingerprint is omitted) or call `current_material_fingerprint` inline (wrapped in try/except per best-effort semantics) and document which is preferred.
findings.finding: The `material` parameter value for the PASS-path sidecar emit in `transition_close.py` is left unresolved — the plan writes `material=…` without specifying what to pass. The material fingerprint is computed inside `_verdict_manifest` (which is called AFTER the proposed emit point in `close_ticket`), so the PASS sidecar either receives `None` (same as the FAIL path, weakening the record) or requires a duplicate `current_material_fingerprint` call. An implementer following the plan literally will have a compile/runtime gap at `material=…`.
findings.location: Scope section: `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` — 'add a best-effort `completion_sidecar.emit(result, material=…, repo_root=repo_root)`'
findings.suggested_fix: Since the PASS emit happens before signing and `_verdict_manifest` already computes the fingerprint, the cleanest fix is to compute it once early: add a best-effort `material = _try_material_fingerprint(ticket_id, repo_root)` helper (wrapping `current_material_fingerprint` in try/except) shared by both the PASS emit and `_verdict_manifest`, or explicitly state `material=None` is acceptable for the PASS sidecar with a follow-up note.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the PASS `completion_sidecar.emit` call goes inside `_completion_precheck` (before `return result`, mirroring the FAIL branch but without material fingerprint) or inside `close_ticket` (after the confirmed close, before or after `_verdict_manifest`, where `material` can be computed); reconcile the two conflicting location anchors in the scope bullet.
findings.finding: The plan locates the PASS-path sidecar emit at `~lines 211-256` of `transition_close.py` (the body of `_completion_precheck`) and says it mirrors the FAIL branch's `emit` call at ~line 238 — but also says it should fire "after the verdict is confirmed PASS, before `_verdict_manifest` signs". `_verdict_manifest` is called inside `close_ticket` (line 441), not inside `_completion_precheck`. `_completion_precheck` merely returns the result to `close_ticket`; the signing and the `_verdict_manifest` call happen later in `close_ticket`. These two location anchors are contradictory, leaving an implementer uncertain whether the emit belongs inside `_completion_precheck` (mirroring the FAIL branch) or inside `close_ticket` (before line 441).
findings.location: `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` scope bullet — '~lines 211-256; the FAIL branch's best-effort `completion_sidecar.emit` is ~line 238'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a unit/integration test for the PASS-path emit failure in `transition_close.py`: mock `completion_sidecar.emit` to raise, invoke the close path, and assert the close succeeds (returns PASS verdict) and the error is swallowed — verifying the 'best-effort' guarantee holds.
findings.finding: The PASS-path emit in `transition_close.py` is described as 'best-effort (swallow its own errors)' but no AC specifies a test that exercises the failure path where `completion_sidecar.emit` raises on the PASS branch — verifying the close still succeeds. This is a new user-facing flow (close operation) covered by happy-path-only test oracles in AC1/AC2.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2 (or a dedicated AC), add an oracle: 'patch completion_sidecar.emit to raise RuntimeError; assert transition_close still returns a PASS result and does not propagate the error.' Mirror the pattern already used (or specify it for the first time) for the FAIL branch's best-effort emit.

findings.finding: The protocol for how `reconcile_verdict` handles the new `criteria[]` field is not co-located with the decision rule that must change. The plan states 'a populated `criteria[]` must NOT trip the PASS-with-findings→FAIL flip' but does not specify whether `reconcile_verdict` should pass `criteria[]` through untouched, validate it, strip it, or do nothing. The current code reads only `result.get('findings')` so the field would be silently ignored — but the plan also says to 'thread `criteria[]` through `reconcile_verdict`', leaving the threading protocol ambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC 3 so a skip is not a silent waiver: either (a) require that the skip be recorded as an explicit deferred-to-named-child ticket with a dependency edge, OR (b) require that when run, the live test is blocking (not advisory) for the first real close — and add a minimum in-session fallback check (e.g., verify the criteria[] JSON structure against the schema even when running offline).
findings.finding: AC 3's live/E2E test is permanently skippable ('skipped with a recorded reason otherwise'), so the new code path where the LLM agent actually emits criteria[] can be bypassed indefinitely. E6(f) requires at least one AC that exercises the cutover path as it will run in production; the three anti-FP exceptions (no live boundary, non-default opt-in flag, explicit deferral to a named child with rationale) do not apply here. A session where API keys are absent can close the ticket with AC 3 silently skipped and the live path never exercised.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 3 ('Live end-to-end: running the real completion verifier...')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to AC 3: 'If skipped due to missing API key, the skip MUST be recorded as a named follow-up ticket (e.g., E2E-live-criteria-validation) and a dependency edge added before this ticket is closed as complete; the skip is not a waiver of the criterion.' Alternatively, restructure AC 3 as [operator-attested] so the live run is attested (with a change-id/timestamp) rather than conditionally skipped.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a scope entry for the E2E test file (e.g. `tests/e2e/test_completion_verifier_live.py` or equivalent), naming the fixture ticket reference and the skip mechanism for non-LLM environments, so AC3 has a covered deliverable.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Live end-to-end: running the real completion verifier on a fixture ticket whose criteria are met produces a PASS with a populated `criteria[]` persisted to the sidecar') names a deliverable — a live/E2E test file — that has no corresponding scope item. None of the eight enumerated edit targets covers creating this test, identifying the fixture ticket, or specifying where the test lives.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC3 / Scope (enumerated edit targets)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: `tests/e2e/test_completion_verifier_live.py` — create (or extend) a live/E2E test module that imports the real `verify_completion` path, points at a designated fixture ticket ID (document the fixture ticket or how to obtain one), calls the verifier with a real API key, and asserts `verdict == 'PASS'` and `len(criteria) > 0` and the sidecar is readable via `latest_pass_record`. Gate with `pytest.importorskip` or a `@pytest.mark.skipif` on missing API key, recording the skip reason.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or update ADR 0043 / CLAUDE.md) documenting: (a) the PASS-path sidecar pattern and why FAIL/PASS use split schema versions, (b) the `completion_verifier_pass_v1` contract shape, (c) the `latest_pass_record` API, and (d) the closed `kind` vocabulary and its source in ADR 0043.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural pattern — PASS-path sidecar emission, a `completion_verifier_pass_v1` schema contract, a `latest_pass_record` API, and a per-criterion audit trail — but no ADR or design-doc update is included in the Scope. ADR 0043 is cited as the authority for the `kind` vocabulary but is not updated to record its use in this new context. Future contributors have no durable record of why PASS and FAIL use split schemas or why a PASS-path sidecar was introduced.
findings.location: Scope section (no doc/ADR edit target listed); 'The `kind` vocabulary' section (references ADR 0043 without updating it); Acceptance Criteria (no AC for doc update)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an entry to the Scope list: `docs/adr/NNNN-completion-pass-sidecar.md` (or an update to ADR 0043) capturing the split-schema rationale, the PASS-path emit pattern, and the `kind` vocabulary. Reference it from the `completion_sidecar.py` module docstring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the best-effort `except` block in the PASS-path sidecar emit logs/warns (e.g., `logger.warning("completion sidecar PASS emit failed", exc_info=True)`) before suppressing the exception, consistent with the observability requirement.
findings.finding: Swallowed errors on the new PASS-path `completion_sidecar.emit` call are not instrumented — the plan specifies 'swallow its own errors' but does not require a log/warn/trace before suppression, leaving operators with no signal when PASS sidecar persistence silently fails.
findings.location: src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py — PASS path sidecar emit; 'Scope (enumerated edit targets)' section, last bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the emit call in `try: completion_sidecar.emit(...) except Exception: logger.warning('completion sidecar PASS emit failed for %s', resolved_id, exc_info=True)` — one line, matches the pattern already used in the FAIL branch if it logs there.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify idempotency semantics for the PASS sidecar write: either state that multiple records per ticket are acceptable and `latest_pass_record` is defined as 'return most-recent' (sufficient), or add a deduplication guard (e.g., check-then-write with ticket_id + fingerprint).
findings.finding: The PASS sidecar write is not idempotent: if `transition_close` is retried or invoked concurrently for the same ticket (e.g., a flaky CI re-run), multiple `completion_verifier_pass_v1` records are written for the same ticket, and the plan neither acknowledges this nor states that accumulation is acceptable.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the plan: '`latest_pass_record` returns the most-recent PASS record for the ticket (last-write-wins); duplicate records from retries are harmless because the schema includes a material fingerprint for deduplication by consumers.' This matches the likely existing `latest_fail_verdict` semantics without code changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a statement to the plan about the RETIRE path for `completion_verifier_pass_v1` records — either 'retained indefinitely as immutable audit log (same policy as FAIL records, no cleanup required)' or specify a TTL/archival mechanism.
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup path is defined for `completion_verifier_pass_v1` sidecar records — the plan introduces a new record type that accumulates per closed ticket but does not state whether records have a TTL, are pruned, or are intentionally retained indefinitely as an audit trail.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to 'Compatibility & rollback': 'PASS sidecar records are retained indefinitely as an immutable audit trail alongside existing FAIL records; no TTL or pruning is defined (same policy as FAIL records).' This closes the T9 lifecycle gap with zero code change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `latest_pass_record` has at least one production call-site (e.g. a CLI inspect command, an MCP tool, or a report reader) before adding it as a public function; if the only consumer is the AC2 test oracle, inline the schema-guarded read into the test itself and defer the public function to when a real consumer is specified.
findings.finding: `latest_pass_record` is introduced in `completion_sidecar.py` with zero declared production call-sites. The plan's only stated consumer is the AC2 integration-test oracle ('`latest_pass_record` reads it back'). No production code path (CLI, MCP, gate, or report command) is specified to call it, making it a YAGNI addition — it is tested infrastructure with no current production use.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `test_fail_close_persists_durable_verdict` and `test_pass_close_leaves_no_fail_record` actually exist before treating them as regression anchors; if they don't (confirmed absent), add them as NEW tests in this plan rather than citing them as pre-existing guards.
findings.finding: The plan cites `test_fail_close_persists_durable_verdict` and `test_pass_close_leaves_no_fail_record` as pre-existing tests that 'stay green' and serve as regression anchors for the FAIL schema and PASS/FAIL sidecar distinction. Neither test exists anywhere in the repo — a repo-wide search returns zero hits for both names.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py' bullet; Schema-version strategy section: 'This keeps `test_fail_close_persists_durable_verdict` and `test_pass_close_leaves_no_fail_record` green'
findings.suggested_fix: Add both test names to the in-scope test list as new tests to be written as part of this work, covering: (1) a FAIL close emits a `completion_verifier_fail_v1` sidecar readable by `latest_fail_verdict`; (2) a PASS close does NOT emit a FAIL-schema record (i.e., `latest_fail_verdict` returns None after a PASS close).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the dual-schema choice in the 'Schema-version strategy' section (e.g., 'two schemas mirror the plan-review sidecar precedent, keep `latest_fail_verdict` zero-churn, and let each reader be schema-typed without a verdict-field dispatch').
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for choosing the dual-schema sidecar approach over a simpler single-schema extension. A structurally different alternative — keeping one schema (`completion_verifier_v2`) for all verdicts, with `criteria[]` optional and `findings[]` optional, differentiated by `verdict` at read-time — would require fewer new symbols (`latest_pass_record` / `SCHEMA_PASS` / `build_payload` branching) and no schema-guard duplication. The plan only argues why the FAIL schema should NOT be bumped (backward compat), but never states why two schemas + two readers + branching `build_payload` is better than one unified schema.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify the choice of sharing one `EVENT_TYPE` for both PASS and FAIL schemas (vs. adding a dedicated `COMPLETION_VERDICT_PASS` event type per the `REVIEW_RESULT` precedent), or adopt the simpler two-event-type approach.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel pattern — one EVENT_TYPE (`COMPLETION_VERDICT`) hosting two structurally different schema-versioned payloads distinguished only by a `schema` field — without considering or dismissing the established alternative: a second, separate event type (e.g. `COMPLETION_VERDICT_PASS`) that follows the existing single-type-per-schema convention set by `REVIEW_RESULT`. The plan's stated benefit (keeping `latest_fail_verdict` untouched) is achievable equally well with a second event type registered in the allow-list and `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`, with no dual-schema dispatch complexity. The novelty is not justified against this prior-art option.

transition_close.py is an unenumerated consumer that must be edited to emit the PASS sidecar. The PASS path currently just calls `_verdict_manifest` and returns; `completion_sidecar.emit()` is only called on the FAIL branch (line 238). Emitting on PASS requires a new call-site on the PASS path in this file, which the Scope does not name.
findings.suggested_fix: In Scope, add: `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` — on the PASS path (after verdict confirmed PASS, before `_verdict_manifest`), call `completion_sidecar.emit(result, material=material, repo_root=repo_root)` with best-effort semantics mirroring the FAIL path.

findings.finding: src/rebar/llm/contracts.py (CompletionVerdict pydantic model) is an unenumerated edit target. The plan requires the verifier prompt to emit a criteria[] array, but the structured-output model at contracts.py:75 has no `criteria` field. The runner validates the LLM's response against this model before reconcile_verdict ever sees it — a model field missing here means the agent's criteria[] is silently stripped before it reaches any downstream consumer.

The completion-verification.yaml workflow and gate_ops.py (completion_reconcile step) are unenumerated consumers that must be updated to thread criteria[] from the agent's output through to the reconciled verdict. The YAML wires only `raw_verdict`, `raw_findings`, `runner`, `model`, `trace_id` from the verify step; `completion_reconcile_input.schema.json` has `additionalProperties: false` with no `raw_criteria` field.

The PASS sidecar emit call site (`transition_close.py`) is absent from the plan's Scope, making the 'emit on PASS' mechanism incomplete: the FAIL emit lives in `transition_close.py` inside the `if verdict != 'PASS'` branch; the PASS branch (lines 242+) has no emit call and is not listed as a file to change.
findings.suggested_fix: In `transition_close.py`, after the FAIL branch's `raise CommandError`, add a best-effort `completion_sidecar.emit(result, material=material_fingerprint, repo_root=repo_root)` call in the PASS continuation (before or after signing). List `transition_close.py` in the plan's Scope.

findings.finding: The verifier prompt (completion_verifier.md) is not updated in the plan to instruct the LLM to emit the new `criteria[]` array. The plan says 'update the verifier prompt to emit it' but provides no specification of what the prompt should say — specifically, what fields to include per entry, how `met` should be expressed, and how `citation` in a criteria entry differs from `citations` in a finding. The `CompletionVerdict` Pydantic model in contracts.py also lacks a `criteria` field; without updating both the prompt and the model, the LLM has no instruction to emit the field and the structured-output contract rejects or ignores it.
findings.location: Scope — `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` / plan phrase 'update the verifier prompt to emit it' / `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py` `CompletionVerdict`

findings.finding: The plan says 'bump schema' in completion_sidecar.py but the existing `latest_fail_verdict` reader guards on `payload.get('schema') == SCHEMA` (the module constant). A schema bump (e.g., to 'completion_verifier_v2') would cause `latest_fail_verdict` to reject all pre-bump FAIL records AND all post-bump records unless the guard is updated. The plan neither specifies the new schema string, nor how `latest_fail_verdict` should handle both old and new schema versions.
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a separate `SCHEMA_PASS = 'completion_verifier_pass_v1'` constant for PASS records rather than bumping the shared SCHEMA, so FAIL records keep their existing schema sentinel and latest_fail_verdict requires no change.

findings.finding: The plan says 'bump schema' in `completion_sidecar.py` but does not specify the new schema string(s) or how the existing `latest_fail_verdict` schema-equality guard (`payload.get('schema') == SCHEMA`) behaves when the constant changes — creating an ambiguous edge case where either (a) the guard silently drops old FAIL records (if `SCHEMA` is renamed) or (b) PASS records carry a semantically misleading `'completion_verifier_fail_v1'` tag.
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a second schema constant (e.g., `PASS_SCHEMA = 'completion_verifier_pass_v1'`) for PASS records, keep `SCHEMA = 'completion_verifier_fail_v1'` unchanged for FAIL records, and have `latest_pass_record` filter on `PASS_SCHEMA`. This preserves the existing FAIL reader guard with zero churn.

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'bump schema' in completion_sidecar.py but leaves the version strategy unresolved. The existing `latest_fail_verdict` reader schema-guards on the hard-coded literal `"completion_verifier_fail_v1"` (completion_sidecar.py line 133). If all newly-emitted COMPLETION_VERDICT events (PASS and FAIL alike) carry a bumped schema string, `latest_fail_verdict` will silently return None for every post-bump FAIL record, breaking the existing caller in `test_fail_close_persists_durable_verdict` and leaving the FAIL observability path dark. The plan names a new `latest_pass_record` reader without specifying whether the old reader is updated, versioned-branched, or whether FAIL events keep the old schema string.
findings.location: Scope section — `src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py` bullet ('bump schema; add a `latest_pass_record` reader'); completion_sidecar.py line 133 (`payload.get("schema") == SCHEMA`)
findings.suggested_fix: Keep `"completion_verifier_fail_v1"` for FAIL events (schema unchanged, reader unchanged, existing test unchanged). Emit PASS events with a new `"completion_verifier_pass_v1"` schema string, read by a new `latest_pass_record` that guards on that literal. This is the minimum-diff path that adds the PASS record without touching any existing FAIL consumer.

findings.finding: The plan says 'bump schema' in completion_sidecar.py but leaves the new schema version string undefined and provides no discrimination protocol for `latest_pass_record`. The existing PASS sidecar reader (`latest_fail_verdict`) schema-guards on the hard-coded literal `"completion_verifier_fail_v1"`. If the PASS sidecar uses the same schema string, `latest_fail_verdict` will return PASS records to callers expecting FAILs; if a new string is used, its value is unspecified. `latest_pass_record` must also distinguish PASS from FAIL events sharing the same `EVENT_TYPE`, but no protocol is given.
findings.location: Scope — `src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py` / plan phrase 'bump schema; add a `latest_pass_record` reader'
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a second schema constant `PASS_SCHEMA = "completion_verifier_pass_v1"` alongside the existing `SCHEMA = "completion_verifier_fail_v1"`, and have `latest_pass_record` guard on `payload.get('schema') == PASS_SCHEMA`.

findings.finding: The plan's Scope section lists three files to modify (completion_verdict.schema.json, completion.py, completion_sidecar.py) and asserts 'emit on PASS as well as FAIL', but omits build_payload — the function emit() directly calls — from the explicit change surface. build_payload's docstring explicitly scopes it to FAIL verdicts ('the slim, queryable sidecar payload for a completion FAIL verdict'), the SCHEMA constant is 'completion_verifier_fail_v1', and the function currently calls reconcile_verdict which strips non-findings fields. Extending emit() to PASS without correspondingly specifying updates to build_payload leaves a critical intermediary unaddressed; a developer following the Scope section as written would produce a PASS-path emit that calls a FAIL-only payload builder, yielding a payload with the wrong schema sentinel and no criteria[] field.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet for completion_sidecar.py, add: 'Update build_payload to accept/emit a per-verdict schema sentinel (completion_verifier_pass_v1 on PASS, completion_verifier_fail_v1 on FAIL) and include criteria[] in the PASS payload; update latest_pass_record to filter on completion_verifier_pass_v1.'

findings.finding: The `kind` field of the new `criteria[]` entry objects is referenced in the plan (`{criterion, met, citation, kind}`) but its allowed value vocabulary is never defined. The plan informally names two kinds (codebase-verifiable / operator-attested) in the verifier prompt, but neither the schema addition to completion_verdict.schema.json nor any part of the plan specifies these as the exhaustive enum, leaving an executor unable to determine valid values for `kind`.
findings.location: Scope — `src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json` / plan phrase `{criterion, met, citation, kind}`
findings.suggested_fix: In the schema addition, use `"enum": ["codebase-verifiable", "operator-attested"]` for `criteria[].kind`, matching the two-way classification already defined in the verifier prompt.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly whether criteria[] should be emitted by the agent on FAIL verdicts (and if so, whether the FAIL sidecar payload should include it or intentionally drop it), so the prompt and sidecar implementation have an unambiguous contract.
findings.finding: The prompt-edit spec for completion_verifier.md does not state whether the agent should emit criteria[] on a FAIL verdict (when some criteria are unmet). The plan's prompt instruction says 'instruct the agent to emit criteria[] with one met-record per evaluated criterion' (implying always), but the sidecar section says 'FAIL → unchanged SCHEMA + findings' with no mention of criteria[] in the FAIL branch. An executor writing the prompt and sidecar cannot determine the authoritative behavior without inferring across sections.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the scope bullet for completion_verifier.md: 'The agent ALWAYS emits criteria[] regardless of verdict (met=false for unmet criteria on a FAIL).' Then add a matching note to the completion_sidecar.py bullet: 'The FAIL payload intentionally omits criteria[] (the FAIL schema is unchanged); criteria[] is computed but not persisted on FAIL — this is by design.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit guidance to the prompt update for the 'nothing to verify' case: 'when there are no concrete completion requirements to verify, emit `criteria: []` (an empty array) — do NOT invent entries'.
findings.finding: The prompt update instruction is silent on what `criteria[]` should contain in the 'nothing to verify' PASS path (a ticket with no concrete requirements). The prompt currently instructs: 'Return PASS with an empty `findings` and a one-line `summary`'. After the update, the agent also needs to know whether to emit `criteria: []` (empty array) or omit `criteria` entirely on this fast-path. Since `criteria` is declared `default_factory=list`, pydantic accepts omission — but the downstream sidecar and test expectations need a consistent definition.
findings.location: Scope section — `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md` edit; 'Nothing to verify' section (prompt lines 149-158)
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Nothing to verify' section of the prompt, append: 'In this case emit `criteria: []` — an empty array (no entries to invent).' This resolves the tension with the general 'one met-record per evaluated criterion' instruction.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit note in the YAML edit spec confirming that `criteria: list[Criterion] = Field(default_factory=list)` (empty list, not None) ensures `steps.verify.outputs.criteria` is always present in the step output dict (exclude_none preserves empty lists), and that the field must NOT be typed `Optional[list] = None`.
findings.finding: The YAML edit threads `raw_criteria: ${{ steps.verify.outputs.criteria }}` into the reconcile step, but the plan does not specify what the workflow engine emits for `steps.verify.outputs.criteria` when the agent omits the optional `criteria` field (default empty list). The verify step is a `prompt:` step with `output_schema: completion_verdict`; the engine exposes fields from the validated pydantic model dump. If the engine only exposes keys present in the actual dict (exclude_none behavior confirmed in findings.py line 313-316), an absent `criteria` means `steps.verify.outputs.criteria` is a missing key — and the YAML expression `${{ steps.verify.outputs.criteria }}` may raise rather than producing an empty list.
findings.location: Scope section — `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/completion-verification.yaml` edit; reconcile `with:` block; also `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_ops.py` edit

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `test_response_model_for_selects_by_output_schema` (line 42 of `test_completion_verifier.py`) to the list of tests that must be updated to include `criteria` in the expected field set, alongside the already-named `test_completion_verdict_pins_to_schema`.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `test_completion_verdict_pins_to_schema` must be updated to include `criteria`, but does not mention the sibling test `test_response_model_for_selects_by_output_schema` (line 42 of the same file), which ALSO hard-asserts `set(cv.model_fields) == {'verdict', 'findings', 'summary'}` and will fail the same way once `criteria` is added to `CompletionVerdict`.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json' bullet ('The model↔schema PARITY test … MUST be UPDATED in lockstep')
findings.suggested_fix: In the scope bullet for `src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json`, append: 'Also update `test_response_model_for_selects_by_output_schema` (line 42) which asserts the same `set(cv.model_fields) == {"verdict", "findings", "summary"}` invariant and will likewise fail.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/interfaces/store/test_completion_verifier.py::test_response_model_for_selects_by_output_schema` (line 42) to the scope's list of tests that must be updated when `criteria` is added to `CompletionVerdict`, alongside the already-named `test_completion_verdict_pins_to_schema`.
findings.finding: `test_response_model_for_selects_by_output_schema` (test_completion_verifier.py line 42) asserts `set(cv.model_fields) == {'verdict', 'findings', 'summary'}` — the same field-set invariant as the named `test_completion_verdict_pins_to_schema` (line 55). Adding `criteria` to `CompletionVerdict` breaks BOTH tests, but only the pin test is listed as a required in-scope edit; the first test is an unenumerated consumer that will also fail.
findings.suggested_fix: In the scope bullet for `contracts.py`, add: 'Also update `test_response_model_for_selects_by_output_schema` (line 42 of the same test file), which contains an identical `set(cv.model_fields) == {"verdict", "findings", "summary"}` assertion.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
